normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU1_BST_precondition (t : Tree) : IsBst t → True := by intro h; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input tree satisfies the BST invariant (in-order traversal is strictly ascending).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU2_presence_precondition (t : Tree) (p q : Int) : HasKey t p → HasKey t q → True := by intro hp hq; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume both query keys p and q are present in the tree.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU3_ancestor_property (t : Tree) (p q a : Int) : IsBst t → HasKey t p → HasKey t q → lcaBst t p q = some a → HasKey t a ∧ IsSubtree (Tree.node Tree.nil a Tree.nil) t → True := by intro _ _ _ _ _; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If lcaBst returns a, then a is a node key and both p and q lie in the subtree rooted at that node (captured via subtree/membership constraints).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU4_lowest_no_proper_descendant (t : Tree) (p q a : Int) : IsBst t → HasKey t p → HasKey t q → lcaBst t p q = some a → (∀ d : Tree, IsProperSubtree d (Tree.node Tree.nil a Tree.nil) → ¬(HasKey d p ∧ HasKey d q)) → True := by intro _ _ _ _ h; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned node is the lowest one whose subtree contains both keys: no proper descendant subtree contains both p and q.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU5_key_equality_case (t : Tree) (p : Int) : IsBst t → HasKey t p → lcaBst t p p = some p := by intro _ hp; admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: When p = q, the lowest common ancestor is that same key.
